claims.allowed_rewrite: Report the baseline result as an observation; do not claim baseline superiority.
claims.claim: A Transformer-based model (e.g., HART) robustly outperforms logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds.
claims.diagnostic: The claim is about logistic regression, but the best observed condition was `candidate`.
claims.next_action: Report which condition won, or run a follow-up focused on why the baseline underperformed.
claims.reason: The claim is about logistic regression, but the best observed condition was `candidate`.

claims.claim: The generated claim should be rejected or rewritten under this protocol: A Transformer-based model (e.g., HART) robustly outperforms logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds.
claims.reason: The verifier found `claim_direction_failed`: The claim is about logistic regression, but the best observed condition was `candidate`.

hypotheses.claim_candidate: A Transformer-based model (e.g., HART) robustly outperforms logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds.
hypotheses.falsification_condition: If logistic regression's mean balanced accuracy is within 1% of Transformer's, or if the effect-to-noise ratio is <1.5, the hypothesis is falsified.
hypotheses.minimum_evidence_needed: One public dataset with 10 seeds, showing Transformer mean balanced accuracy > logistic regression by at least 2% and effect-to-noise ratio >2.
hypotheses.why_it_matters: HAR literature (arxiv_2209_11750_2022) shows Transformers outperform CNNs/LSTMs and are robust to sensor heterogeneity, suggesting a nonlinear advantage over logistic regression.

lab_question.question: Can a nonlinear model robustly outperform logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds?
success_criteria: external dataset discovery finds a usable Hugging Face or OpenML dataset
the research protocol is generated from the question, literature, and dataset audit
the experiment code is generated, statically validated, dry-run, executed, analyzed, verified, and reported

protocols.claim_blocked_if: mean balanced_accuracy of best candidate model is within 1% of logistic regression mean balanced_accuracy or effect-to-noise ratio < 1.5
protocols.claim_enabled_if: mean balanced_accuracy of best candidate model > mean balanced_accuracy of logistic regression by at least 2% and effect-to-noise ratio > 2 across 10 seeds
protocols.scientific_goal: A Transformer-based model (e.g., HART) robustly outperforms logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds.
